data.title: UK authorities consider giving fraud victims $6.4 billion from seized 61K Bitcoin, not original value.
data.description: Authorities in the United Kingdom are currently considering whether to retain around $6.4 billion in profits acquired from Bitcoin that was confiscated in connection to a significant fraud scheme originating from China. The funds in question were seized as part of the investigation into the case, and officials are deliberating whether to keep the sum rather than returning it to the victims who were affected by the scheme.

data.title: Sweden considers creating its own national Bitcoin reserve as proposed by two lawmakers, following global trend.
data.description: A proposal from two members of the Sweden Democrats aims to align the country's policies with those of nations such as the United States regarding Bitcoin reserves. This motion reflects the growing trend of countries considering holding Bitcoin assets.

data.title: Wolters Kluwer wins several awards for finance, risk, regulatory excellence. Awards from Risk Asia Awards 2025 and Global BankTech Awards 2025.
data.description: In Singapore, Wolters Kluwer Financial & Corporate Compliance has recently been awarded prestigious accolades, solidifying its position as a top provider of finance, risk, and regulatory reporting software solutions for global financial institutions. The company received recognition from leading industry awards programs - the Risk Asia Awards 2025 and The Digital Banker’s Global BankTech Awards 2025. This reaffirms Wolters Kluwer's leadership role in the sector. The company expressed gratitude for the honors received, emphasizing the importance of these achievements within the industry.

data.title: ExQor Technologies in Boston is moving towards clinical trials for brain regenerative medicine to treat Alzheimer's disease.
data.description: In Boston on October 2, 2025, ExQor Technologies, located in Boston, MA, is gearing up for clinical trials for its unique brain regenerative medicine platform designed to tackle Alzheimer's disease, a pressing medical issue. The company aims to address the critical unmet needs of patients suffering from Alzheimer's. This initiative comes as a significant step forward in the fight against the disease, affecting millions worldwide.

data.title: The Longest Table, supported by Nextdoor Foundation, expands across the U.S. to connect neighbors through meals.
data.description: In an initiative to tackle loneliness and foster stronger community bonds, The Longest Table expands its efforts in San Francisco. The program aims to bring more neighbors together through shared meals, emphasizing the importance of local connections.

data.title: Akamai & Apiiro expand partnership for application security. Akamai (NASDAQ: AKAM) announces collaboration with leading agentic security platform.
data.description: Akamai Technologies, headquartered in Cambridge, Massachusetts, revealed on October 2, 2025, a significant development in its collaboration with Apiiro, a prominent agentic application security platform. This expanded partnership with the leading cloud company, listed on NASDAQ as AKAM, signifies a pivotal step in enhancing online security measures. The strategic alliance with Apiiro aims to fortify digital protection for users, solidifying Akamai's position in safeguarding online activity.

data.title: Bimergen Energy secures $50M for battery energy storage project development with new joint venture partnership.
data.description: On October 2, 2025, Bimergen Energy Corporation, a company specializing in utility-scale battery energy storage solutions, revealed that it has obtained a $50 million capital pledge to expand its projects. The funding was secured through a collaboration between Bimergen's subsidiary, Emergen Energy LLC, and a battery energy storage systems manufacturer, which will operate as a joint venture. This initiative aims to enhance Bimergen's existing portfolio and support further development in the field of independent power provision.

data.title: Global Virtual Clinical Trials Market expected to reach USD 15.8B by 2034 with 5.8% CAGR from USD 8.8B in 2024. Key drivers include decentralized trials, AI/IoT integration, and digitalization via telemedicine. Market leaders improving automation & cloud apps. Regulatory compliance and innovation in North America & Europe, while Asia-Pacific shows fastest growth due to investments & government support. Strategic partnerships enhance competitiveness in the market.
data.description: A research report titled "Virtual Clinical Trials Market" projects that the market size was valued at approximately USD 8.8 Billion in 2024, expected to reach USD 8.9 Billion in 2025 and grow to around USD 15.8 Billion by 2034, with a CAGR of about 5.8% during the forecast period. The market is driven by factors like decentralized trials, AI and IoT integration, and digitalization supporting telemedicine initiatives. Strong demands for decentralized trials, increased use of AI and IoT for real-time monitoring and predictive analytics, and digital telemedicine adoption are key trends. The report covers market size, key segments, regions, competitive landscape, growth factors, and recent trends, with buying options for tailored research requirements. It includes an in-depth industry analysis, updated regional analysis for 2025, top market players' strategies, and a modified COVID-19 impact study. Strategic partnerships between technology providers, sponsors, and research organizations are enhancing innovation, efficiency, and competitiveness in the global Virtual Clinical Trials Market.

data.title: Smiles on the Upperwest Side has moved to 15 West 96th Street, 4th Floor, Manhattan, quadrupling their space.
data.description: On September 22, 2025, Smiles on the Upperwest Side moved to a cutting-edge dental facility at 15 West 96th Street, 4th Floor in New York. This relocation marks a significant change from its previous 97th Street base to a larger space, over four times the size. The new premises promise improved services and advanced dental care for clients in the area.

data.title: 4Rivers Equipment becomes Trimble Technology Outlet, selling products in Colorado, New Mexico, and parts of Texas.
data.description: A John Deere dealer in Westminster, Colorado is now offering Trimble Technology to customers in Colorado, New Mexico, and parts of Texas. On October 2, 2025, Trimble announced that 4Rivers Equipment, based in Greeley, Colorado, has become the latest Trimble Technology Outlet. Customers in these areas can now purchase Trimble products from 4Rivers Equipment.
